title: Strategy-economics features from optimal stint length and undercut condition
summary: Public F1 strategy references frame pit timing as a trade-off between degradation slope and pit-loss, with a first-order optimal stint length near sqrt(2P/d) and an explicit undercut inequality.
rationale: Public F1 strategy references frame pit timing as a trade-off between degradation slope and pit-loss, with a first-order optimal stint length near sqrt(2P/d) and an explicit undercut inequality. That is a cleaner feature family than fixed per-compound stop windows.
implementation_hint: Estimate a local degradation rate d from causal lap-history or current wear proxies, assign a race-level pit-loss prior P, then derive features like Optimal_Stint_Length, TyreLife_minus_Optimal, RemainingLaps_minus_OptimalFresh, and an Undercut_Advantage proxy based on old-tyre next-lap loss versus fresh-tyre recovery minus pit loss. Use race-specific shrinkage priors rather than hard-coded regex buckets only.
expected_effect: Should especially help middle-stint dry-race decisions where the model must choose between undercut, overcut, and extending.
risk: P and d are latent and noisy; bad pit-loss priors can create sharp but wrong features.
